title: Pungent Charred German Sausage Skillet
description: A one-pot dish featuring charred German sausage, leafy greens, and a rich buttermilk sauce, all cooked to perfection in a single skillet.

ingredients.name: 4 German sausages (Bratwurst or Knockwurst)
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality sausages for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons butter
ingredients.notes: Use unsalted butter for a milder flavor.

ingredients.name: 1 large onion
ingredients.notes: Chop the onion into bite-sized pieces.

ingredients.name: 2 cloves garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced garlic adds depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: 1 cup leafy greens
ingredients.notes: Choose your favorite leafy greens, such as kale or spinach.

ingredients.name: 1 cup buttermilk
ingredients.notes: Use full-fat buttermilk for a richer sauce.

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon caraway seeds
ingredients.notes: Caraway seeds add a traditional German flavor.

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt for a cleaner flavor.

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black pepper adds a nice kick.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Garnish with fresh parsley for a pop of color.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup chopped fresh chives
ingredients.notes: Garnish with fresh chives for an oniony flavor.

ingredients.name: 2 cups mixed mushrooms
ingredients.notes: Choose your favorite mushrooms, such as button or cremini.

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onions to the skillet and cook until they are caramelized and slightly charred.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced sausages to the skillet and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the mixed mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they are tender and slightly browned.
Add the leafy greens to the skillet and cook until they are wilted.
In a small bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the buttermilk mixture over the sausages and greens in the skillet.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer the mixture for 5-7 minutes, until the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the chopped parsley and chives.
Serve the Pungent Charred German Sausage Skillet hot, garnished with additional parsley and chives if desired.
